sub_graphs.name: cell_to_coset_evals
invariants: INV-001: Each cell byte segment [start:end] is exactly BYTES_PER_FIELD_ELEMENT bytes
INV-002: Output CosetEvals has exactly FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_CELL elements

sub_graphs.name: coset_evals_to_cell
invariants: INV-001: Output Cell has exactly FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_CELL * BYTES_PER_FIELD_ELEMENT bytes
INV-002: Inverse of cell_to_coset_evals for valid field elements

sub_graphs.name: _fft_field
invariants: INV-001: len(vals) must be a power of 2
INV-002: len(vals) == len(roots_of_unity)
INV-003: Recursive calls halve the input size at each level

sub_graphs.name: fft_field
invariants: INV-001: When inv=True, applies inverse FFT normalization factor 1/N
INV-002: Inverse FFT reverses roots_of_unity order (except first element)

sub_graphs.name: coset_fft_field
invariants: INV-001: shift_factor = PRIMITIVE_ROOT_OF_UNITY
INV-002: Forward coset FFT shifts then FFTs; inverse FFTs then unshifts
INV-003: Coset avoids division by zero when dividing by vanishing polynomials

sub_graphs.name: compute_verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch_challenge

Act: for commitment in commitments: hashinput += commitment
E: for commitment in commitments: hashinput += commitment

invariants: INV-001: Fiat-Shamir heuristic: all verifier-relevant data is included in hash input
INV-002: Domain separator RANDOM_CHALLENGE_KZG_CELL_BATCH_DOMAIN prevents cross-protocol attacks
INV-003: Hash input order is deterministic and canonical

sub_graphs.name: polynomial_eval_to_coeff
invariants: INV-001: Input polynomial has exactly FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_BLOB evaluations
INV-002: Bit-reversal permutation must be applied before inverse FFT

sub_graphs.name: add_polynomialcoeff

Act: order a, b so len(a) >= len(b)
result[i] = a[i] + (b[i] if i < length_b else 0)
E: order a, b so len(a) >= len(b)
result[i] = a[i] + (b[i] if i < length_b else 0)

invariants: INV-001: Result degree = max(deg(a), deg(b))
INV-002: Shorter polynomial is zero-padded for missing coefficients

sub_graphs.name: multiply_polynomialcoeff

Act: summand = [0]*power + [coef * x for x in b]
E: summand = [0]*power + [coef * x for x in b]

invariants: INV-001: Product degree <= FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_EXT_BLOB - 1
INV-002: Result degree = deg(a) + deg(b)

sub_graphs.name: divide_polynomialcoeff

Act: a[diff + i] = a[diff + i] - b[i] * quot
E: a[diff + i] = a[diff + i] - b[i] * quot

invariants: INV-001: b must not be the zero polynomial
INV-002: a = quotient * b + remainder (remainder is discarded)
INV-003: Leading coefficient of b (b[bpos]) must be nonzero

sub_graphs.name: interpolate_polynomialcoeff

Act: for j in range(len(ys)), j != i
E: for j in range(len(ys)), j != i
for j in range(len(ys)), j != i

invariants: INV-001: All xs values must be distinct (for invertibility)
INV-002: Result polynomial has degree at most len(xs) - 1
INV-003: evaluate(result, xs[i]) == ys[i] for all i

sub_graphs.name: vanishing_polynomialcoeff
invariants: INV-001: Result polynomial evaluates to 0 at every point in xs
INV-002: Result has degree exactly len(xs)
INV-003: Leading coefficient is 1 (monic polynomial)

sub_graphs.name: evaluate_polynomialcoeff
invariants: INV-001: Uses Horner's method for O(n) evaluation
INV-002: Result equals sum of coeff[i] * z^i for all i

sub_graphs.name: compute_kzg_proof_multi_impl

Act: ys = [evaluate_polynomialcoeff(poly, z) for z in zs]
E: ys = [evaluate_polynomialcoeff(poly, z) for z in zs]

invariants: INV-001: Q(X) = f(X) / Z(X) where Z vanishes on all evaluation points zs
INV-002: deg(Q) = deg(f) - deg(Z) = deg(f) - len(zs)
INV-003: Proof is a commitment to the quotient polynomial

sub_graphs.name: verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch_impl

Act: assert lengths match and commitments unique
E: assert lengths match and commitments unique

invariants: INV-001: Verification equation: e(LL, LR) == e(RL, [1])
INV-002: Random challenge r binds all inputs via Fiat-Shamir
INV-003: Commitments list must contain no duplicates
INV-004: All commitment_indices must reference valid entries in commitments
INV-005: Universal verification equation aggregates multiple cell proofs into single pairing check

sub_graphs.name: coset_shift_for_cell
invariants: INV-001: cell_index must be in [0, CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB)
INV-002: Returns the coset generator h for h*G where G is subgroup of order FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_CELL

sub_graphs.name: coset_for_cell
invariants: INV-001: cell_index must be in [0, CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB)
INV-002: Returned Coset has exactly FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_CELL elements
INV-003: Coset elements are h*G = {h, hg, hg^2, ...} where h = coset_shift_for_cell(cell_index)

sub_graphs.name: compute_cells

Act: ys = [evaluate_polynomialcoeff(poly_coeff, z) for z in coset]
E: ys = [evaluate_polynomialcoeff(poly_coeff, z) for z in coset]

invariants: INV-001: Input blob must be exactly BYTES_PER_BLOB bytes
INV-002: Output contains exactly CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB cells
INV-003: Each cell represents polynomial evaluations over a distinct coset

sub_graphs.name: compute_cells_and_kzg_proofs_polynomialcoeff
invariants: INV-001: Output contains CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB cells and proofs
INV-002: Each proof is valid for its corresponding cell and coset
INV-003: O(n^2) complexity; FK20 algorithm preferred for production

sub_graphs.name: compute_cells_and_kzg_proofs
invariants: INV-001: Public method: accepts raw blob bytes
INV-002: Blob must be exactly BYTES_PER_BLOB bytes
INV-003: Delegates to compute_cells_and_kzg_proofs_polynomialcoeff after conversion

sub_graphs.name: verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch

Act: assert all input lists same length
for each commitment_bytes assert len == BYTES_PER_COMMITMENT
for each cell assert len == BYTES_PER_CELL
for each proof_bytes assert len == BYTES_PER_PROOF
E: assert all input lists same length
for each commitment_bytes assert len == BYTES_PER_COMMITMENT
for each cell assert len == BYTES_PER_CELL
for each proof_bytes assert len == BYTES_PER_PROOF

invariants: INV-001: Public method: accepts raw bytes and performs cryptographic normalization
INV-002: All four input lists must have equal length
INV-003: Commitment deduplication preserves semantic equivalence
INV-004: Cell indices must be in [0, CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB)
INV-005: Implements universal verification equation from ethresear.ch

sub_graphs.name: construct_vanishing_polynomial
invariants: INV-001: At least one cell must be present (not all can be missing)
INV-002: Vanishing polynomial evaluates to 0 at all missing cell evaluation points
INV-003: Coefficient spacing by FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_CELL extends polynomial to full domain

sub_graphs.name: recover_polynomialcoeff

Act: build extended_evaluation_rbo with zeros for missing cells
ez_eval = [a * b for a, b in zip(zero_poly_eval, extended_evaluation)]
reconstructed = [a / b for a, b in zip(ez_over_coset, zp_over_coset)]
E: build extended_evaluation_rbo with zeros for missing cells
ez_eval = [a * b for a, b in zip(zero_poly_eval, extended_evaluation)]
reconstructed = [a / b for a, b in zip(ez_over_coset, zp_over_coset)]

invariants: INV-001: E(x) * Z(x) == P(x) * Z(x) over the FFT domain
INV-002: Division by Z(x) is done over a coset to avoid division by zero
INV-003: Recovered polynomial has degree < FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_BLOB
INV-004: At least 50% of cells must be available for successful recovery

sub_graphs.name: recover_cells_and_kzg_proofs

Act: for each cell assert len == BYTES_PER_CELL
E: for each cell assert len == BYTES_PER_CELL

invariants: INV-001: Public method: accepts raw cells and indices
INV-002: At least CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB / 2 cells required (50% threshold)
INV-003: Cell indices must be unique and in ascending order
INV-004: All cell indices must be in [0, CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB)
INV-005: Recovery uses Reed-Solomon erasure coding with FFT-based reconstruction

sub_graphs.name: get_validators_custody_requirement
invariants: INV-001: Return value is always >= VALIDATOR_CUSTODY_REQUIREMENT (8)
INV-002: Return value is always <= NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS
INV-003: total_node_balance is non-negative (sum of effective balances)

sub_graphs.name: cells_and_kzg_proofs_construction
invariants: INV-001: Each blob produces exactly CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB cell-proof pairs
INV-002: blobs_bundle.proofs has length CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB * len(blobs_bundle.blobs)
INV-003: Proof slicing indices [start:end] are always in bounds

sub_graphs.name: get_data_column_sidecars
invariants: INV-001: len(cells_and_kzg_proofs) == len(kzg_commitments) (precondition)
INV-002: Exactly NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS sidecars are produced
INV-003: Each sidecar contains one cell and one proof per blob
INV-004: DataColumnSidecar.index == column_index for each sidecar

sub_graphs.name: get_data_column_sidecars_from_block
invariants: INV-001: signed_block must be a valid SignedBeaconBlock
INV-002: Merkle proof is computed over blob_kzg_commitments field in block body
INV-003: Delegates to get_data_column_sidecars with extracted components

sub_graphs.name: get_data_column_sidecars_from_column_sidecar
invariants: INV-001: len(cells_and_kzg_proofs) == len(sidecar.kzg_commitments) (precondition)
INV-002: Reuses header, commitments, and inclusion proof from the existing sidecar
INV-003: Delegates to get_data_column_sidecars for full sidecar reconstruction

sub_graphs.name: sidecar_publishing
invariants: INV-001: Each sidecar is published to exactly one subnet
INV-002: subnet_id is deterministically computed from column_index
INV-003: All sidecars are published before completion

sub_graphs.name: sidecar_retention
invariants: INV-001: Sidecars MUST be retained for MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS epochs
INV-002: Sidecars MUST be served when requested during retention period
INV-003: Pruning is optional (MAY) only after retention period expires
